props.covers: Per-shape visual and connection reference: every canvas object type at its default creation geometry, with computed connection-anchor positions and text-slot behavior, each illustrated by a live canvas embed.

text.insert: A per-shape visual and connection reference: every one of the 17

values at its default creation geometry, with its real computed connection-anchor positions and its text-slot behavior, each illustrated by a live canvas embed. This bundle builds on the model layer's field-by-field object type reference in
— schema fields, tones, and validation rules are documented there, not repeated here — and on the shape API design doc
. What this reference adds is the connection-facing view: where connectors actually attach on each shape, and where each shape puts its text.
(10 world px) outside the object's bound, then projects each along the ray from the object's center onto the shape's real outline via
. True outlines are modeled for rect/rounded-rect, pill, diamond, and arrow-shape; every other type falls back to its axis-aligned bounding rect. During an interactive connect,
snaps the pointer to the nearest cardinal anchor within
(8 view px, divided by zoom), else to the nearest outline point within
(8 world px), else connects to the shape's center when the pointer is inside it, else falls through to a free point.
Every embed below follows the same recipe: the subject shape sits at its
markers outside it. Each marker's connection endpoint stores BOTH the coarse side anchor and the exact
computes for that side — so the rendered connector lands on the true outline anchor, not a hand-guessed point. On most shapes those four points coincide with the bounds-edge midpoints; on

the top/bottom anchors sit visibly inside the bounding box, on the chevron body's edges. A fifth free-floating text object under each shape states the text-slot rule (centered vs. top-left vs. below-icon vs. body-only).
-based grouping box. Default creation geometry is
, sized to hold a few default shapes. The label renders top-left in the shared base chrome (
), with body text below it; nested children render as their own objects on top.
Anchors treat this type as a plain rect outline (
's fallback), which matches the drawn rounded rect.
resolves the four cardinal anchors to
top (260, 80) = [0.5, 0]; right (440, 200) = [1, 0.5]; bottom (260, 320) = [0.5, 1]; left (80, 200) = [0, 0.5] (world point = relative [rx, ry] on the bounds).

polygon over the full bounding box. Label and body render centered, with extra padding since a diamond's usable interior is smaller than its box.

Note the four cardinal anchors still coincide with the bounds-edge midpoints — a diamond's north/south/east/west vertices sit exactly on its bounding box. The true outline only diverges from the box off-axis, which is what the outline-snap stage of
sees when a pointer approaches a diamond edge diagonally.
Free-floating label text, FigJam's plain-text tool. Takes the catch-all default
boldLabel), transparent background, no border, and no rendered body.

field renders as a chip pinned bottom-left at 12px in 40% black (

collection. Default geometry is a small square,
, zero padding) with the label centered on it.

The drawn silhouette is not modeled as a true outline — anchors fall back to the axis-aligned bounds rect (the same conservative fallback the upstream BlockSuite port uses for icon-like shapes), though at a square 40×40 the four cardinal midpoints lie on the drawn circle anyway.

A file/document node (D16 vocabulary). Default geometry
, drawn as a dog-eared page via CSS
with an asymmetric border radius; label renders top-left, body below, in the shared base chrome.

The drawn silhouette is not modeled as a true outline — anchors fall back to the axis-aligned bounds rect (the same conservative fallback the upstream BlockSuite port uses for icon-like shapes), so the clipped top-right fold corner is inside the anchor rect — a connector aimed at the top-right lands on the box, not the fold.

The drawn silhouette is not modeled as a true outline — anchors fall back to the axis-aligned bounds rect (the same conservative fallback the upstream BlockSuite port uses for icon-like shapes), so anchors land on the box around the glyph, not on the head/shoulders path itself.

The drawn silhouette is not modeled as a true outline — anchors fall back to the axis-aligned bounds rect (the same conservative fallback the upstream BlockSuite port uses for icon-like shapes), so anchors land on the box, not the curved cylinder caps.

field is still required by validation but is not separately rendered — the chip is the visible title. The embed uses the

As with the diamond, the four cardinal anchors coincide with the bounds-edge midpoints — a stadium's extremes sit on its bounding box — so the true outline only matters off-axis, where outline snapping follows the end-cap curves instead of the box corners.
The fat chevron (W2), pointing left or right via the type-only
matches the measured reference proportions. Drawn as a single SVG polygon tracing the 7-point silhouette from
(head 38% of width, body 53% of height). The label centers within the chevron BODY only — an offset margin equal to the head width keeps text off the pointed side.
The showcase true-outline case: anchors project onto the actual chevron polygon, and the connector attachment outline is the very same
resolves the four cardinal anchors to
The top and bottom anchors land on the chevron body's horizontal edges at

— visibly inset from the bounding box (the body is 53% of total height, vertically centered, so each inset is (1 − 0.53) / 2 = 0.235). Left lands on the body tail, right on the chevron tip. This embed is the clearest demonstration that connectors attach to the true outline, not the bbox.
The flowchart "predefined process" rect (W2): a rounded rect with two inner vertical bars near each end, signaling a called subroutine. Default geometry
; bars are 4px wide, inset 4.7% of total width from each edge per
Anchors treat this type as a plain rect outline (
's fallback), which matches the drawn rounded rect (the side bars are interior decoration, not outline).

(same footprint as person), drawn via the SVG
: a rounded square body with four pins per side, filled with

The drawn silhouette is not modeled as a true outline — anchors fall back to the axis-aligned bounds rect (the same conservative fallback the upstream BlockSuite port uses for icon-like shapes), so anchors land on the box around the glyph — including the pin stubs, which reach the box edges.
